info.name: SmartBed API
info.description:
# SmartBed Backend API

Production-grade REST API for the SmartBed sleep and health monitoring platform.

## Quick Start
1. Set `base_url` variable to your server (default: `http://localhost:8080`)
2. Call **POST /auth/login** with admin credentials
3. Copy the `access_token` from the response — Postman auto-sets it via the login test script
4. All authenticated routes automatically use the `{{access_token}}` variable

## Roles
| Role | Access |
|---|---|
| `admin` | Full access |
| `doctor` | Protocols, device commands, clinical read |
| `caregiver` | Clinical read, ack alerts |
| `operator` | Vitals ingestion |
| `patient` | Own profile |

## Base URL
`{{base_url}}/api/v1`

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description: Authentication endpoints. Login returns a JWT access token that is automatically stored and used by all other requests.

request.description:
Authenticates a user and returns a JWT access token + refresh token.

**Roles:** Public (no auth required)

**Test accounts after seeding:**
- `admin@smartbed.local` / `Admin@2024!`

**Auto-sets:** `{{access_token}}` and `{{user_id}}` collection variables

Liveness probe for load balancers and Kubernetes readiness checks.

**Roles:** Public

item.name: 📡 Vitals Ingestion
item.description: Receives biometric data payloads from Apple Watch / iPhone bridge. Normalises into time-series, deduplicates, and triggers async risk evaluation.

request.description:
Main ingestion endpoint called by the Apple Watch bridge every N seconds.

**Roles:** `operator`, `admin`

**What happens internally:**
1. Raw payload stored in `raw_ingestion_log`
2. Each metric normalized into `vital_events` (idempotent — duplicate device+timestamp+metric is silently ignored)
3. Async risk evaluation triggered per metric
4. If HR + movement high → composite sleep disturbance evaluated
5. Alerts created and notifications routed based on risk level

**Metrics (all optional):**
| Field | Unit | Clinical bounds |
|---|---|---|
| `heart_rate` | bpm | 60–100 (varies by age/sex) |
| `spo2` | % | 95–100 (< 92 = HIGH risk) |
| `stress_level` | 0–100 score | 0–40 |
| `sleep_duration_minutes` | min | 360–540 |
| `skin_temperature` | °C | 34.5–37.5 |
| `movement_score` | 0–1 float | 0–0.4 |
| `sleep_stage` | `light`/`deep`/`rem`/`awake` | — |

Simulates a critical event:
- SpO₂ = 89% → **CRITICAL** (hard threshold < 90%)
- Heart rate = 110 bpm → elevated
- Temperature = 38.5°C → elevated (> 38°C = HIGH risk)
- Movement = 0.85 → very high

**Expected result:** 3 alerts created (low_oxygen CRITICAL, abnormal_heart_rate, elevated_temperature), caregiver + doctor + emergency contacts notified.

Simulates disturbed sleep:
- Heart rate elevated during sleep
- Movement score high

**Expected:** `disturbed_sleep` alert (composite rule triggers when HR > 110% of normal AND movement > 75% of max bound)

item.name: 🚨 Alerts
item.description: Health alerts generated by the risk engine. Alerts are auto-created when metric deviations exceed thresholds and are routed to caregivers/doctors based on severity.

request.description:
Returns paginated list of alerts for a patient.

**Roles:** `admin`, `doctor`, `caregiver`

**Alert types:**
| Type | Trigger |
|---|---|
| `abnormal_heart_rate` | HR outside age/sex range |
| `low_oxygen` | SpO₂ < 95% (critical < 90%) |
| `elevated_temperature` | Skin temp > 38°C |
| `disturbed_sleep` | High HR + high movement |
| `high_risk_health_event` | Risk 10–20% |
| `critical_health_event` | Risk > 20% |

**Risk levels:** `normal` → `mild` → `high` → `critical`

response.name: 200 OK
response.body:
{
  "alerts": [
    {
      "id": "aaaa1111-bbbb-cccc-dddd-eeeeeeeeeeee",
      "user_id": "{{user_id}}",
      "alert_type": "low_oxygen",
      "risk_level": "critical",
      "message": "spo2: 89.00 (below normal, risk=critical, deviation=-9.00)",
      "metric_type": "spo2",
      "metric_value": 89,
      "is_acknowledged": false,
      "acknowledged_by": null,
      "acknowledged_at": null,
      "created_at": "2026-03-08T02:15:01Z"
    },
    {
      "id": "bbbb2222-cccc-dddd-eeee-ffffffffffff",
      "user_id": "{{user_id}}",
      "alert_type": "abnormal_heart_rate",
      "risk_level": "high",
      "message": "heart_rate: 110.00 (above normal, risk=high, deviation=37.00)",
      "metric_type": "heart_rate",
      "metric_value": 110,
      "is_acknowledged": false,
      "acknowledged_by": null,
      "acknowledged_at": null,
      "created_at": "2026-03-08T02:15:01Z"
    }
  ],
  "count": 2
}

request.description: Filter to only unacknowledged alerts. Useful for the dashboard notification badge count.

Marks an alert as acknowledged by the currently authenticated user.

**Roles:** `admin`, `doctor`, `caregiver`

Sets `is_acknowledged=true`, records `acknowledged_by` (user UUID) and `acknowledged_at` timestamp.

item.name: 📊 Dashboard
item.description: Read-only endpoints for the web dashboard and mobile caregiver app. Returns aggregated health status, sleep summaries, and risk histories.

request.description:
Returns a snapshot of the patient's current health status:
- Unread alert count
- Last 10 unread alerts
- 7-day sleep summary

**Roles:** `admin`, `doctor`, `caregiver`

**Ideal for:** Dashboard home card, caregiver app top-of-screen status widget.

Returns aggregated sleep statistics over the last N days.

**Roles:** `admin`, `doctor`, `caregiver`

**Score explanation:**
- **Quality Score**: 0–100 composite (duration + HR stability + SpO₂ stability + disturbances + movement)
- **Disturbed nights**: sessions with score < 60 OR ≥ 3 disturbances

**Query params:**
- `days`: `7` (default) or `30`
30-day sleep trend. Useful for the monthly report card and doctor consultation view.

item.name: 🏥 Protocols
item.description:
Doctor-configured medication and response workflow management. Protocols define what happens when a patient's vitals trigger specific conditions.

⚠️ **Safety note:** Only doctors can create protocols. The `send_dispenser_command` outcome requires multiple safety checks before it can execute.

request.description:
Creates a new protocol in **draft** state.

**Roles:** `doctor` only

**Trigger outcomes:**
| Value | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `notify_only` | Sends notification, no device action |
| `suggest_action` | Creates a dashboard suggestion card |
| `require_manual_approval` | Creates a pending approval record — must be manually approved before any action |
| `send_dispenser_command` | Issues command to IoT dispenser **only after** all 5 safety gates pass |

**State machine:** `draft` → `approved` → `active` → `suspended`

**Rules operators:** `gt`, `lt`, `gte`, `lte`, `eq`

**Metric types:** `heart_rate`, `spo2`, `skin_temperature`, `stress_level`, `movement_score`, `sleep_duration`, `respiration`, `blood_pressure_systolic`, `blood_pressure_diastolic`

response.name: 201 Created
response.body:
{
  "id": "cccc3333-dddd-eeee-ffff-000000000000",
  "patient_id": "{{user_id}}",
  "doctor_id": "dddd4444-eeee-ffff-0000-111111111111",
  "name": "Nocturnal Hypoxia Watch",
  "description": "Notify caregiver if SpO2 drops below 93% during sleep hours",
  "state": "draft",
  "trigger_outcome": "notify_only",
  "doctor_approved": false,
  "is_locked": false,
  "created_at": "2026-03-08T22:45:00Z",
  "updated_at": "2026-03-08T22:45:00Z"
}

request.description:
Creates a dispenser-triggering protocol.

⚠️ **Safety gates** that MUST all pass before a dispenser command is issued:
1. Protocol state = `active`
2. `doctor_approved` = `true`
3. `is_locked` = `false`
4. All protocol rules match the incoming risk evaluation
5. A real online dispenser device must be registered

Protocol starts in `draft` — must be advanced through the state machine.

Advances protocol from **draft → approved**.

**Roles:** Doctor who created the protocol only

This sets `doctor_approved = true` on the protocol. Only the owning doctor may approve.
Activates an approved protocol. Once active, the risk engine will evaluate incoming vitals against its rules.
Immediately suspends an active protocol. No further dispenser commands or triggered notifications will fire until reactivated.

item.name: 🤖 IoT Device Commands
item.description:
MQTT-backed dispenser device command lifecycle management.

**Command lifecycle:**
```
queued → published → acknowledged → executed
                  ↘ failed
queued → timeout (after ACK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
```

Commands are published to MQTT topic: `devices/{device_code}/commands`
Device ACKs come back on: `devices/{device_code}/ack`

request.description:
Issues a command to a registered dispenser device. Published via MQTT.

**Roles:** `doctor`, `admin`

**Command types:**
| Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `dispense` | Trigger physical dispensing of configured medication |
| `hold` | Suppress next planned dispense |
| `reset` | Reset the dispenser state machine |
| `check_status` | Ping device for a status heartbeat |

**MQTT flow:**
1. Command stored as `queued`
2. Published to `devices/{device_code}/commands` → status: `published`
3. Device ACKs on `devices/{device_code}/ack` → status: `acknowledged` → `executed` or `failed`
4. If no ACK within `ACK_TIMEOUT_SECONDS` → status: `timeout`

⚠️ Will fail with 400 if device is offline or not found.

Returns device registration info and its most recent command with lifecycle status.

**Roles:** `admin`, `doctor`

**Useful for:** Device dashboard card, real-time command tracking widget.

response.name: 404 Not Found

request.description:
This is NOT an HTTP endpoint — it is documentation for the MQTT ACK message format.

**Topic:** `devices/{device_code}/ack`
**Direction:** Device → Server

**ACK Payload (JSON):**
```json
{
  "command_id": "eeee5555-ffff-0000-1111-222222222222",
  "device_id": "disp_001",
  "status": "executed",
  "message": "Dose dispensed successfully",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-08T22:45:12Z"
}
```

**Status values:** `executed` | `failed`

**Testing with MQTT Explorer or mosquitto_pub:**
```bash
mosquitto_pub -h localhost -p 1883 \
  -t 'devices/disp_001/ack' \
  -m '{"command_id":"COMMAND_UUID","device_id":"disp_001","status":"executed","message":"OK","timestamp":"2026-03-08T22:45:12Z"}'
```

item.name: 📐 Risk Engine – Reference
item.description:
Documentation folder showing how the risk engine classifies incoming vitals. Not HTTP endpoints — these are example payloads with annotated risk calculations for frontend developers.

**Risk formula:**
```
Deviation      = Current_Value − Normal_Value
Risk_Pct       = |Deviation / Normal_Value| × 100
Risk_Level     = normal (0–5%) | mild (5–10%) | high (10–20%) | critical (>20%)
```

**Hard overrides (bypass percentage rule):**
- SpO₂ < 90% → always `critical`
- SpO₂ < 92% → always `high`
- SpO₂ < 95% → at least `mild`
- Temp ≥ 39°C → always `critical`
- Temp ≥ 38°C → at least `high`

**Composite disturbance rule:**
IF HeartRate > 110% of normal AND MovementScore > 75% of max THEN `disturbed_sleep` alert

request.description:
**Example calculation for SpO₂ = 89%**

Assume patient: age 35, male → normal SpO₂ = 98%

```
Deviation    = 89 - 98        = -9
Risk_Pct     = |−9 / 98| × 100 = 9.18%
Classified   = mild (by %)     → OVERRIDDEN
Hard rule    = SpO₂ < 90      → CRITICAL
Final level  = CRITICAL
```

**Alert created:** `low_oxygen` / `critical`
**Notifications:** caregiver + doctor + all emergency contacts

item.name: Risk Calc Example – Heart Rate 110 bpm (HIGH)

request.description:
**Example calculation for HR = 110 bpm**

Assume patient: age 35, male → normal HR = 71 bpm

```
Deviation    = 110 - 71        = +39
Risk_Pct     = |39 / 71| × 100 = 54.9%
Classified   = CRITICAL (>20%)
```

**Alert:** `abnormal_heart_rate` / `critical`
**Notifications:** caregiver + doctor + emergency contacts

item.name: Risk Calc Example – Normal Sleep (no alert)

request.description:
**Example: HR = 68 bpm (normal)**

Assume patient: age 35, male → normal HR = 71 bpm

```
Deviation    = 68 - 71        = -3
Risk_Pct     = |−3 / 71| × 100 = 4.22%
Classified   = NORMAL (<5%)
```

**No alert created.** Vital event stored for historical trend only.

item.name: 🛠 Admin – User Management
item.description:
Administrative endpoints for creating users and managing profiles.

**Note:** Full CRUD for users is implemented in the service layer. These document the intended request shapes for frontend admin panels.

request.description:
Creates a new user account.

**Roles:** `admin`

**Available roles:**
- `admin` — system administrators
- `doctor` — attending physicians
- `caregiver` — family members / nurses
- `operator` — IoT bridge / device integration service accounts
- `patient` — the monitored person

Password is bcrypt-hashed (cost 10) before storage.

response.name: 409 Conflict – Email taken

item.name: 💡 Environments & Variables
item.description:
Collection variables reference for frontend developers.

| Variable | Set by | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `base_url` | Manual | Server URL (e.g. `http://localhost:8080`) |
| `access_token` | Login test script | JWT Bearer token (auto-set) |
| `user_id` | Login test script | Authenticated user UUID (auto-set) |
| `protocol_id` | Create Protocol test script | Last created protocol UUID |
| `device_id` | Manual | Registered dispenser device UUID |
| `alert_id` | Get Alerts test script | First unread alert UUID |

**Environments to create in Postman:**

**Local:**
```
base_url = http://localhost:8080
```

**Staging:**
```
base_url = https://staging-api.smartbed.io
```

**Production:**
```
base_url = https://api.smartbed.io
```
